text.title: AI IaC Hallucination Detector Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules with rationale + source for ai-iac-hallucination-detector. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every `resource "TYPE"` MUST match a type listed in the active provider schema. Unknown types are fabrications and MUST cause `verdict=fail`.
rule.rationale: Terraform docs — provider schema is the authoritative type list; type errors at `plan` are catchable earlier.

rule.statement: Every attribute inside a resource block MUST exist in the schema's attribute list for that resource. Typos and invented attributes block merge.
rule.rationale: Terraform provider SDK — unknown attributes raise `Unsupported argument` at plan; pre-plan check shortens the loop by 2-5 minutes per PR.

rule.statement: `module "NAME" { source = ... }` paths MUST resolve to a published module in the Terraform Registry, a reachable git ref, or a local `./` path that exists in the repo.
rule.rationale: Terraform Registry docs — fabricated sources fail at `init` but waste runner time and leak hallucinated names into logs.

rule.statement: `data "TYPE"` blocks MUST match the schema's data-source list, same rigor as resources.
Resources marked `deprecated` in the schema MUST be replaced before merge unless the PR carries `migration-in-progress` label.
rule.rationale: Provider deprecations precede removal by 1-2 minor versions; merging deprecated resources guarantees a follow-up break.

rule.statement: If the change is documentation-only or targets an in-house provider without a published schema, return `skipped`.
